qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Template Injection in Twig formula evaluation allows arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes server-side template injection in Twig formula evaluation, which does not match the advisory-described SQL injection in the purchasables table sorting endpoint. The advisory specifies SQL injection via the sort parameter passed directly to orderBy() in the purchasables table endpoint, while this finding describes template injection in a different component (discount/shipping rule formula evaluation). The root cause mechanisms differ: missing SQL injection sanitization in the sort parameter vs missing Twig delimiter validation in evaluateFormula().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding does not match the advisory-described SQL injection vulnerability in Craft Commerce purchasables table sorting. The advisory (GHSA-j3x5-mghf-xvfw) describes SQL injection where the sort parameter is split by | and the first part (column name) is passed directly as an array key to orderBy() without whitelist validation, allowing an authenticated attacker to inject arbitrary SQL into the ORDER BY clause. The finding 'ssti_twig_formula_injection' describes server-side template injection in Twig formula evaluation for discount/shipping rules - a different vulnerability class (SSTI vs SQL injection), different endpoint, and different root cause (missing Twig delimiter validation vs missing SQL injection sanitization in sort parameter handling).
evidence: Advisory GHSA-j3x5-mghf-xvfw describes SQL injection in Craft Commerce purchasables table sorting via the sort parameter
Advisory identifies the vulnerable code as missing whitelist validation for the sort parameter column name passed to orderBy()

Finding 'ssti_twig_formula_injection' describes SSTI in Twig formula evaluation for discount/shipping rules - different vulnerability class (SSTI vs SQL injection), different endpoint, and different root cause (missing Twig delimiter validation vs missing SQL injection sanitization)
The finding does not mention the purchasables table endpoint, the sort parameter handling, or the orderBy() SQL injection vulnerability
